Indonesia must industrialize sustainably for the benefit of its people
Education is key to shaping Indonesia's future
Indonesia must develop its own technology and expertise
Collaboration and diplomacy are essential for success
Family is the foundation of Anindya's success
The importance of not underestimating ASEAN
episodes.quotes: Di keluarga tuh dibilang enggak ada yang sempurna, kayak jari tangan. Sudah ada jempol, ada telunjuk, jari tengah, tapi kalau hilang satu ya enggak bisa genggam.

episodes.title: Telco to Techco: Turning the Digital Table Upside-Down - Vikram Sinha | Endgame #156
episodes.guest_title: President Director and CEO of Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison
episodes.summary: Vikram Sinha discusses his upbringing in India, his career in telecommunications, and his experience leading Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison through a major merger. He emphasizes the importance of cultural understanding, empathy, and a focus on customer experience in achieving success. He also highlights the role of digital infrastructure and generative AI in Indonesia's future.
key_highlights: Success comes from values instilled during upbringing
Indonesia is more than just Jakarta, Surabaya, or Bali
English is a tool to perform better
Gotong Royong is key to Indonesia's DNA
It's okay to fail, but don't repeat the same mistakes
You need to have healthy disrespect for your own decisions
It's important to connect all your employees to the purpose
episodes.quotes: Indonesia is not just Jakarta, Surabaya, or Bali, you know. It is not only that nickel to steel story, there is much more and many more things. The talent, the young digital native, you know, all those things stack up on Indonesia's side.

episodes.title: Is Climate Studies the New Computer Science? - Arun Majumdar | Endgame #155 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Arun Majumdar discusses the importance of sustainability and climate change mitigation, emphasizing the need for a cultural shift and the role of governments in creating a supportive environment. He highlights the need for a holistic approach that considers both micro and macro levels, and the importance of empowering local communities to create solutions tailored to their specific needs. He also touches on the role of nuclear energy as a potential alternative energy source.
key_highlights: The future grid will be about energy plus synergy, provided by information technology
Bits and watts: digital and power are the keys to the future
Education is a big deal, and it is a means to get ahead
It's important to be open-minded about climate change solutions
The world needs talent in other parts of the world
It's important to meet people where they are today
We need an international FEMA for climate events
episodes.quotes: The bits is for digital, and watts is for the power. In the future, it's going to be about energy plus synergy, and that is provided by information technology.

episodes.title: 7 Lessons from a 137 Year Old Brand - John Murphy | Endgame #154 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: President and Chief Financial Officer of The Coca-Cola Company
episodes.summary: John Murphy discusses Coca-Cola's long-term strategy, emphasizing sustainability, community engagement, and innovation. He highlights the importance of building trust-based relationships and a diverse portfolio to navigate challenges. Murphy stresses the need for action over mere planning, advocating for a mindset of constant improvement and a commitment to hard work.
key_highlights: There's no substitute for hard work, and it's important to stay curious
Coca-Cola is committed to being water-neutral in its operations
Coca-Cola has been in Indonesia since 1927
The world belongs to the discontented
It's important to be part of the community
It's important to have people you can trust
episodes.quotes: There's no substitute for hard work. And I think sometimes along the way the ability to deliver what's expected of you in the here and now sometimes gets lost.

episodes.title: Gotong Royong untuk Tiga Nol Keberlanjutan Indonesia | Endgame #153
episodes.guest_title: Tana Lee Sullivan, Head of Sustainability, GoTo Group; Denica Riadini-Flesch, Founder & CEO, SukkhaCitta; Gita Syahrani, Head of Secretariat, Lingkar Temu Kabupaten Lestari (LTKL)
episodes.summary: This episode of Endgame discusses the challenges of achieving sustainability goals in Indonesia, emphasizing the need for a balance between profit and purpose. The guests highlight the importance of investing in human capital and education to drive sustainable practices. They also discuss the need to move beyond a narrow focus on carbon emissions and consider the broader material footprint of economic activities, while acknowledging the importance of cultural context and consumer behavior in promoting sustainable choices.
key_highlights: Sustainability efforts must be scalable across the entire ecosystem
Balance profit with purpose to create meaningful impact
Measuring impact and transparency are critical for credible sustainability
It's wrong to take more than nature can replenish
Strongest defense against technology gone awry is humanity itself
episodes.quotes: Enggak ada yang salah dari kita mengambil. Yang salah adalah ketika kita mengambil lebih dari apa yang alam bisa mengembalikan. Jadi this notion of kita itu bagian dari alam dan apa yang kita ambil, kita kembalikan.

episodes.title: Jalan Panjang dan Sunyi Saintis | Endgame #152
episodes.summary: This episode of Endgame discusses the importance of STEM education in Indonesia, the need for long-term thinking and investment in research and development, and the role of the Indonesian diaspora in advancing the country's scientific capabilities. The speakers emphasize the need to move beyond short-term gains and to foster a culture of scientific thinking and innovation in order to compete on a global scale. They also discuss the importance of having a clear vision and strategy for education and research, and the need for collaboration and knowledge exchange between different institutions and individuals.
key_highlights: Indonesia needs long-term investment in research and development, not just short-term gains
Indonesia is not yet a key player on the global map
Indonesia needs to build a culture of scientific thinking and innovation
Indonesia needs to improve its PISA scores in language and STEM
The Indonesian diaspora can play a key role in helping Indonesia
Indonesia needs to think long-term and not just short-term
Indonesia needs to have a clear vision and strategy
episodes.quotes: Kita selalu berpikir jangka pendek. Kita pengen dapat fast return. Sementara research, research and development it's never fast return dan banyak sekali investasi yang mungkin nggak balik, tapi kalau we are not starting to think to invest ya nggak akan nggak akan pernah kita mulai dan nggak akan pernah kita leading.

episodes.title: Topang Bangsa Lewat Perangkat Lunak - Suwandi Soh | Endgame #151
episodes.summary: Suwandi Soh discusses his personal journey from Medan to Jakarta, emphasizing the importance of digitalization for Indonesian SMEs to fully leverage AI. He highlights the need for a shift in mindset and culture to embrace technology, particularly in sectors like agriculture and fisheries. Soh advocates for increased investment in education and infrastructure to bridge the gap between Indonesia and more developed nations in Southeast Asia.
key_highlights: Digitalization is the first step to maximizing the benefits of AI
Integration of components is the second step
Deploy AI to achieve business goals
Education is key to increasing productivity
Indonesia's productivity is low compared to other countries
Mindset and culture need to be changed to embrace technology
Technology can help to increase productivity
episodes.quotes: Jadi step satu itu menurut saya untuk benar-benar dapetin manfaat maksimal dari AI itu adalah digitalisasi. Itu step satu. Step kedua itu integrasi antar komponen. Betul. Baru step ketiga, bagaimana deploy AI untuk mencapai tujuan bisnisnya.

episodes.title: Ekologi, Ekonomi & Lomba Menuju Cukup - Gita Syahrani | Endgame #150
episodes.guest_title: Head of Secretariat of LTKL (Lingkar Temu Kabupaten Lestari)
episodes.summary: Gita Syahrani discusses the importance of regulations and SOPs, but emphasizes that the people implementing them must understand and be motivated. She shares her personal journey, highlighting the influence of her grandmother's love for nature and the importance of defining "enough" in the context of Indonesia Emas 2045. She also touches on the need for a shift in mindset towards sustainability.
key_highlights: Regulations are only effective if people understand and are motivated to follow them
Indonesia Emas 2045: Redefining "enough" in the context of personal circumstances
Importance of a shift in mindset towards sustainability
The need to connect with one's roots and culture
The importance of individual change in driving systemic change
episodes.quotes: Kalau teman-teman di dalam manusianya ini belum sepenuhnya paham dan belum sepenuhnya tergerak, maka kalau bahasa hukumnya itu itu bisa mati secara sosiologis gitu, Pak. Jadi ada sih barangnya, tapi enggak ada yang pakai.

episodes.title: What’s Next for Southeast Asia’s Longest-Running Literature Festival? | Endgame #149
episodes.guest_title: Founder & Director, Ubud Writers & Readers Festival and Ubud Food Festival; Puteri Indonesia 2022
episodes.summary: The host and guests discuss the importance of literacy and culture in Indonesia, the role of technology in shaping the future, and the need for more sustainable actions. They explore the challenges of balancing tradition with modernity and the importance of critical thinking and emotional intelligence. The guests also touch on the need for more Indonesian voices to be heard on the global stage.
key_highlights: Literacy is more than reading; it's understanding important issues
Ubud Writers Festival promotes reading culture and antidotes short sound bites
Writing is a form of communication with others and ourselves
Need for emotional capacity, not just cognitive, to understand important issues
The internet has elitized society; top 0.1% control disproportionately
Need to balance technology with spirituality to understand our purpose
History of art is important to broaden our perspective and use our IQ
episodes.quotes: "To understand philosophy is digging deep within ourselves by not just reading but also writing. It's a form of us to communicate not just to other people but to ourselves."

episodes.title: Monopoli atau Kompetisi: Masa Depan Health-tech Indonesia | Endgame #148
episodes.guest_title: Reynazran Royono, Founder and CEO Fita; Jessy Abdurrahman, Founder and CEO Zi.Care; Jockie Heruseon, VP Business Development and Inovasi Telkomsel
episodes.summary: Gita Wirjawan hosts a panel discussion on the future of health-tech in Indonesia. The panel discusses the challenges of democratizing data, the importance of government regulation, and the need for a shift in focus from curative to preventative healthcare. They emphasize the need for collaboration and open-source research to improve the health sector in Indonesia, while also acknowledging the cultural and regulatory barriers that exist.
key_highlights: Indonesia’s health sector contributes only 5% to GDP, indicating room for growth
Indonesia’s health sector loses 170 trillion rupiah annually to medical tourism
Building trust in local healthcare facilities is key to retaining Indonesian patients
Data privacy regulations are essential for responsible innovation in health-tech
The focus should shift from curative to preventative healthcare to improve public health
Collaboration and open-source research are vital for advancing health-tech in Indonesia
episodes.quotes: Orang Indonesia itu cenderung menggunakan platform-platform kesehatan yang lebih sifatnya telemedicine. Berarti udah telat. Orang itu udah sakit duluan, baru aware about kesehatan.

episodes.title: Kakak Luna Maya si Penakluk Ombak - Tipi Jabrik | Endgame #147
episodes.summary: Tipi Jabrik discusses his journey as a surfer and the potential of surfing in Indonesia. He emphasizes the importance of infrastructure, telecommunications, and a strong local community for developing surfing destinations. Jabrik also highlights the need for Indonesia to focus on quality tourism and to leverage its natural assets to attract international surfers.
key_highlights: Indonesia has a slogan about being a seafaring nation, but many can't swim
Beachfront property is prime location in Australia and California
Family background: father from Jakarta, grandfather was a soldier
Parents met in Bali at a hangout spot called Kayu Api
Surfing is more than a lifestyle, it's my life
He is a calculated risk taker, not reckless
The most important thing is trust
episodes.quotes: "We have a proverb that our ancestors were sailors, but many of our coastal people can't swim. Many don't like the sea."

episodes.title: Dunia Distopia Baskara Putra @hindia | Endgame #146
episodes.summary: Baskara Putra discusses the commodification of mental health issues in art, the feeling of powerlessness in the face of global crises, and the importance of critical thinking and historical awareness. He expresses concern about the increasingly dystopian outlook of his generation, the dominance of sensationalism and fiction over fact, and the erosion of trust in institutions. He emphasizes the need for a balance between state and society and for artists to use their platform to promote social justice.
key_highlights: Artists now commodify mental health issues, selling them as a marketable topic
Many feel we're living on a sinking ship
Powerlessness is felt in politics, climate, inflation, and AI displacement
The best diplomats are athletes and artists
The best way is to be critical, and to think about this
episodes.quotes: "Banyak orang-orang seumuran saya dan sedikit ke atas atau bawa banyak ke bawah itu udah merasa yang kayak we're living on a sinking ship aja right now. We feel powerless banget."

episodes.title: Dua Milenium yang Hilang dari Indonesia - Asisi Suhariyanto | Endgame #145
episodes.summary: Asisi Suhariyanto discusses his childhood interest in Indonesian culture, particularly wayang, and how it led him to a career in media. He shares his experience working as a caricaturist during the 1998 Indonesian crisis, which taught him the importance of being careful with narratives. Asisi also reflects on his travels in Bosnia and Israel, and how they shaped his understanding of Indonesian history and identity.
key_highlights: History is not just data, it is the story of people who lived
History should be felt, not just understood
Culture is a powerful tool for education
Indonesia's strength is its diversity and tolerance
Indonesia has a unique blend of Hindu-Buddhist and local traditions
episodes.quotes: Di balik sejarah, itu ada manusia. Sejarah bukan cuma data. Di sana, di masa lalu, ada manusia-manusia yang bernafas. Manusia yang jantungnya berdetak seperti kita. Ada ketakutan. Ada kerinduan pada pasangan. Ada kegalauan. Ada raja-raja yang mempunyai titik jatuh.

episodes.title: Indonesians Cracking the Code of Silicon Valley - Kevin Chandra | Endgame #144
episodes.summary: Kevin Chandra shares his journey from Surabaya to Silicon Valley, emphasizing the importance of education and the unique challenges he faced as an Indonesian immigrant. He discusses the role of AI in enhancing human productivity and expresses hope for a future where open-source models are more prevalent. He also touches on the importance of maintaining a strong team and adapting to new technologies.
key_highlights: Fail fast, fail cheap, fail forward during youth
Education is a superpower
AI enhances human productivity
Open-source AI models are important
The future is personalized
The importance of a strong team
episodes.quotes: Waktu selagi masih muda, kamu tuh mesti kayak fail fast, fail cheap, fail forward.

episodes.title: Dr. Yasantha: AI vs AGI & Homo Sapiens’ Next Chapter | Endgame #143 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Yasantha discusses the impact of AI on humanity, noting that AI is trained on the entire corpus of human knowledge, but lacks a model of the world. He argues that AI will increase productivity, but at the expense of the environment. He also raises the prospect of AI being used to manipulate people's minds.
key_highlights: AI is trained on the entire corpus of human knowledge, but lacks a model of the world
AI is like a puppy dog, trying to please you
AI is going to further exacerbate inequality
AI is the new colonialism
AI will allow people to get out of the rat race
AI will use more energy
episodes.quotes: People who have AI is like the new colonialists. They will be able to impose their will. And I think in the future people will fall in love with this AI and many things will happen.

episodes.title: Saving Democracy from China, Russia, AI, and Ego - Larry Diamond | Endgame #142 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Senior Fellow at the Hoover Institution and Stanford University
episodes.summary: Larry Diamond argues that democracy is in a recession due to the allure of lies over truth and the rise of authoritarian regimes. He emphasizes the need for a multi-pronged approach to defend democracy, including promoting human rights, economic development, and the rule of law. Diamond expresses concern about China's growing influence and the potential for conflict in the South China Sea.
key_highlights: Democracy is in recession due to the allure of lies over truth
Development must be sustainable, just, organic, and broadly distributed
The US needs to be a reliable partner for democracies
China seeks regional hegemony and wants the US out of Asia
The US needs a pragmatic and hard-headed foreign policy
The rule of law is a political construct, not just legal
Social media has malignant properties that undermine democracy
episodes.quotes: Lies are more spectacular, they're more alluring than the good old boring truth, often. | The democratic recession began around 2006, 2007. | We need to strike a better balance between principle and pragmatism.

episodes.title: Karlina Supelli: Cipta, Rasa, Karsa Manusia Indonesia | Endgame #141 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Karlina Supelli argues that education should focus on building critical thinking, imagination, and creativity, rather than competitiveness. She emphasizes the importance of cultivating emotional intelligence and understanding one's purpose in the world. Supelli also critiques the narrow definition of nationalism and the overemphasis on standardized curricula, advocating for a more open and culturally sensitive approach to education.
key_highlights: Education should focus on building critical thinking, imagination, and creativity
Education should cultivate emotional intelligence and understanding one's purpose
Education should not be focused on competitiveness, but on human development
Indonesian education needs more open and culturally sensitive approaches
The role of the teacher is more important than the curriculum
Education should cultivate a sense of wonder and curiosity
Education should help people to understand their emotions
episodes.quotes: "Jadi jangan berpusat di daya saingnya, tapi berpusat di manusia-manusia yang mau dibangun memiliki daya kritis, memiliki imajinasi, sangat kreatif."

episodes.title: Darren Walker: Justice in the Eyes of the Privileged | Endgame #140 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Darren Walker discusses the role of philanthropy in addressing inequality, the importance of empathy in leadership, and the challenges of navigating a complex world. He emphasizes the need for critical thinking, historical awareness, and a commitment to justice. He also highlights the importance of empowering marginalized communities and ensuring that technology serves the common good.
key_highlights: Technological innovation can exacerbate inequality, but empathy is key to success
Listen to build bridges, find consensus, and common ground
80% of biodiversity is controlled by indigenous people
Listen to and center the voices of indigenous people
Philanthropy should not overlook the economic injustice that makes it necessary
episodes.quotes: Empathy is a critical ingredient to succeed in the world and to also lead and manage in a very complicated changing dynamic world.

episodes.title: Solusi Iklim Tertimbun di Bawah Kaki Kita? - Amando Lasabuda | Endgame #139
episodes.guest_title: Researcher at the Arctic University of Norway
episodes.summary: Amando Lasabuda discusses his background and research on paleogeography and climate modeling in the Arctic. He emphasizes the importance of reliable data for climate modeling and expresses skepticism about brain drain, arguing that Indonesians abroad can still contribute to their homeland. He also touches on energy transition, the challenges of achieving carbon neutrality, and the importance of education and collaboration.
key_highlights: Reading something multiple times is key to understanding complex topics
Education is a crucial investment for a better future
Trust and responsibility are key values instilled from a young age
Reliable paleogeography data is essential for accurate climate modeling
Brain circulation is better than brain drain for national development
Indonesia needs more open access to geological data
Collaboration and open-mindedness are crucial for progress
episodes.quotes: "Kalau sekalian enggak ngerti, dua kali baca. Masih enggak ngerti, baca tiga kali. Masa dalam sepuluh kali baca enggak ngerti juga kan?"

episodes.title: Sabrang: Imajinasi Buatan dan Pengembaraan Kemanusiaan | Endgame #138
episodes.summary: Sabrang argues that AI's impact on society is complex, and that a focus on value-driven education is essential to navigate the future. He contends that while AI offers opportunities for efficiency, it also poses risks to human agency and cultural diversity. He emphasizes the importance of human connection, critical thinking, and a focus on intrinsic motivation over metrics-driven outcomes.
key_highlights: AI's impact is multi-layered, requiring a focus on human values and ethics
Economic metrics can distort education, prioritizing quantity over quality
Indonesia's rich cultural diversity is a strength in the face of globalization
The most valuable education happens in the home, not the classroom
AI's power to personalize knowledge access is a double-edged sword
The future requires a balance between technology and human connection
episodes.quotes: Adaptation is the only currency. You have to know what's happening to be able to adapt. It's survival of the fittest.

episodes.title: James Robinson: The World Is Rebalancing | Endgame #137 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor at the Harris School of Public Policy at the University of Chicago
episodes.summary: James Robinson discusses the importance of institutions for long-term prosperity, arguing that good leadership alone is insufficient. He critiques the term "artificial intelligence" as a misnomer, emphasizing the need for a fundamental shift in mindset and values to define what constitutes a good life. Robinson also touches on the role of social media in distorting perceptions and the challenges of achieving sustainable development in developing economies.
key_highlights: Great leadership needs institution building to last; good leaders alone are not enough
Artificial intelligence is a misnomer; it's artificial something, but not intelligence
A fundamental change in mindset and values is needed for a good life
Social media distorts perceptions, undermining the ability to build inclusive institutions
The world is rebalancing; Africa has enormous potential but needs better institutions
The US has high social mobility, but inequality is moving toward Latin American levels
The unintended consequences of technology depend on the institutional context
episodes.quotes: You can have great leadership, but unless that leads to institution building, great leadership won't last. You know, you can't rely on good leaders. You need to build institutions, you know, that's the key.

episodes.title: John Mearsheimer: Is China the Real Winner of Ukraine War?  | Endgame #136 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Political Science Professor at the University of Chicago
episodes.summary: Mearsheimer argues that the war in Ukraine is likely to be protracted, benefiting China as the US is distracted. He believes the US policy of liberal hegemony and NATO expansion has provoked Russia, leading to the current conflict. He also predicts that the US will increasingly focus on East Asia, particularly Taiwan, due to China's growing power, potentially leading to future conflicts.
key_highlights: The war in Ukraine is likely to be protracted, benefiting China
A cold peace between Russia and Ukraine is the best-case scenario
The US policy of liberal hegemony has provoked Russia
The US is now reverting to a Cold War-like situation with China
The US is distracted in Europe, allowing China to rise
The US may use nuclear weapons to rescue a losing conflict with Russia
The US should focus on East Asia, not Ukraine
episodes.quotes: The war in Ukraine is likely to go on for a long, long time. The Chinese are the winners so far in this war.

episodes.title: Antropolog Kupas Aceh, Bahaya Mesos, dan Pemimpin Ideal - Reza Idria | Endgame #135
episodes.guest_title: Lecturer at UIN Ar-Raniry Banda Aceh and Fellow at NUS Stanford University
episodes.summary: Reza Idria discusses his background in Aceh, his education in Islamic studies, and his current work in anthropology. He argues that Indonesia has a narrative of always being "baik-baik saja" (all good) and that this can lead to dehumanization. He also discusses the importance of critical thinking and the dangers of over-reliance on social media.
key_highlights: Indonesia's narrative of always being "baik-baik saja" can lead to dehumanization
Importance of critical thinking and questioning narratives
Overconfidence and superiority can lead to dehumanization
The need to promote Aceh as a halal tourism destination
Social media polarizes conversations and prioritizes feelings over empirical evidence
The importance of family and community in Indonesian culture
episodes.quotes: Masalahnya Indonesia ini kan narasinya selalu baik-baik saja. Enggak ada musuh yang kecuali musuh kita itu di dalam. Kita itu ya karena kepentingan harmoni tadi, itu tanpa sadar kita itu betul-betul dalam posisi orang yang melakukan dehumanisasi.

episodes.title: Michael McFaul: Ukraine and Showdown Between Democracy & Autocracy | Endgame #134 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Director of the Freeman Spogli Institute at Stanford University, Former US Ambassador to Russia
episodes.summary: McFaul argues that the war in Ukraine is not about NATO expansion or military threats from the West, but about threats from democracy. He contends that Putin fears democratic expansion more than NATO expansion. McFaul also asserts that the West should have done more to alleviate poverty in Russia after the fall of the Soviet Union, as this created an association between democracy and depression.
key_highlights: The war in Ukraine is about threats from democracy, not NATO expansion
Putin fears democratic expansion more than NATO expansion
Most Russians associate democracy with depression
The West should have done more to alleviate poverty in Russia
The war in Iraq is not a defense for Putin's actions
episodes.quotes: I don't think, you know, the war in Ukraine is not about NATO expansion. It's not about threats from military threats from the West. It's about threats from democracy.

episodes.title: How South Korea Deals with Brain Drain - Gi-Wook Shin | Endgame #133 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Director of APARC at Stanford University
episodes.summary: Gi-Wook Shin discusses the factors that contributed to South Korea's economic and political success, including a strong emphasis on education, a willingness to engage with the outside world, and the importance of political leadership. He cautions that South Korea's success is not guaranteed, and that the country faces several challenges, including a declining birth rate, an aging population, and political polarization. He argues that South Korea must embrace cultural diversity and attract foreign talent to maintain its competitive edge.
key_highlights: Brain drain can be converted into brain circulation and linkage
Political leadership is crucial for a country's success
Korea's success is a miracle
South Korea needs to embrace cultural diversity
South Korea needs to attract foreign talent
Korea's political polarization is a concern
The value of education is important
episodes.quotes: Take a chance, but think about how you can convert brain drain into something else, either circulation or linkage.

episodes.title: Taiwan Invasion: How Soon and How to Deter It? - Oriana Skylar Mastro | Endgame #132 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Courtesy Assistant Professor of Political Science at Stanford University
episodes.summary: Oriana Skylar Mastro discusses the need for the US to maintain a strong military presence in Asia to deter China, but also to avoid actions that would provoke China into action. She argues that the US should focus on conventional military capabilities and that China's desire for regional hegemony is a greater threat than its desire to control Taiwan. She also notes that the economic relationship between the US and China is a deterrent to military action.
key_highlights: The US should trade nuclear capabilities for China downgrading conventional capabilities
The US should have a blanket policy of willingness to talk
China's military build-up is a concern
The US needs to be the security partner of choice
Southeast Asia punches below its weight
China wants neutrality from other countries
Deterrence is expensive, but war is more expensive
episodes.quotes: One thing I think some people overseas don't understand is how much the United States actually believes its narratives about sort of protecting those that can't protect themselves.

episodes.title: Dr. Omar Suleiman: Bridging Beliefs, Rediscovering Islam | Endgame #131 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Islamic Studies at Southern Methodist University
episodes.summary: Dr. Omar Suleiman discusses his upbringing as a Palestinian refugee in Louisiana, the importance of family and community, and the need to reclaim Islam from negative stereotypes. He emphasizes the need for Muslims to engage in interfaith dialogue and to build bridges with people of other faiths. He also highlights the importance of social justice and the need to stand up for the oppressed.
key_highlights: Islam is often intentionally misunderstood to scare people
Family has been deprioritized in our discourse
Never burden the oppressed to come up with the solution
Language of peace is often used to oppress
We need to tell our story from the start
episodes.quotes: You never burden the oppressed to come up with the solution. You've got your foot on my neck, remove your foot from my neck and we can talk. The language of peace is often used to oppress.

episodes.title: Green Chemist: AI Utopia and Beyond Periodic Table - Martyn Poliakoff | Endgame #130 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Research Professor of Chemistry at the University of Nottingham
episodes.summary: Martyn Poliakoff explores the relationship between chemistry and sustainability, highlighting the need for efficient use of elements and the potential of AI to discover new chemical combinations. He emphasizes the importance of changing public attitudes and behaviors towards the environment and the role of education in promoting sustainable practices. He also touches on the challenge of balancing economic growth with environmental concerns.
key_highlights: Robin Hood question: How to give to the poor without robbing the rich?
Efficient use of elements can improve the standard of living.
The best way to inspire children in science is to forbid it.
AI can help discover new chemical combinations for sustainable solutions.
Changing public behavior is harder than changing attitudes.
The key is to focus on the system as a whole.
The goal is to improve the quality of life for the poorest.
episodes.quotes: I have formulated what I call the Robin Hood question, which is how can we give to the poor without robbing the rich? So what we have to do is, given the reserve of elements that we have in the world, is to use them more efficiently.

episodes.title: The Trilemma: Between Energy, Ecology, and Economy - Roland Horne | Endgame #129 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Energy Science and Engineering at Stanford University
episodes.summary: Roland Horne discusses the trilemma of energy, ecology, and economy, focusing on the need for a transition away from fossil fuels. He notes that the demand for energy will likely outpace the shift towards renewables, and that the economics of energy production are key to sustainability. Horne also highlights the importance of technological innovation and the need for a balanced approach to energy policy.
key_highlights: Energy consumption is increasing, and this will overpower the move away from fossil fuels
The expectation of energy consumption will shrink as we go forward
Geothermal energy is a good alternative to fossil fuels
Nuclear energy is a sensible idea, but there are concerns
The US has gone full circle twice on nuclear energy
Developing nations need access to capital to develop geothermal energy
Renewables are not as reliable as fossil fuels
episodes.quotes: "The force of people, you know, wanting to improve their life by increasing their energy consumption is going to overpower the force of people who want to move away from fossil fuels."

episodes.title: Perempuan Indonesia Pemilik Paten AstraZeneca: Carina Joe | Endgame #128
episodes.guest_title: Senior Scientist at Jenner Institute, University of Oxford
episodes.summary: Carina Joe discusses her childhood in Jakarta, her education, and her career path. She describes her experience as a scientist at the Jenner Institute at Oxford, focusing on vaccine development. She shares her insights on the challenges and rewards of working in the field of biotechnology, and the importance of having a supportive environment.
key_highlights: Indonesian women often feel inferior compared to their counterparts from other countries
Pandemic is not new, but humans never learn from it
Lacked a clear career path until a biology class in high school
Chose University of Hong Kong because of the guardian program
The most difficult thing is that people don't learn from pandemics
The most important thing is to have an impact on people
episodes.quotes: “Jadi yang banyak saya observe dari orang Indonesia, female, perempuan, itu mereka sering merasa rendah diri dibandingkan dengan counterpart yang lain. Mereka tidak berani speak up.”

episodes.title: Kiyoteru Tsutsui: Japan Economic Diplomacy and Minority Rights Activism | Endgame #127 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Sociology at Stanford University
episodes.summary: Kiyoteru Tsutsui analyzes the relationship between Japan's economic diplomacy and its human rights activism, particularly concerning minority rights. He argues that Japan's approach differs from the US, prioritizing rule of law and economic benefits over direct promotion of democracy. He also notes that Japan's cautious approach to China is influenced by its geographical proximity and economic interdependence, leading to a focus on building trust and offering assistance.
key_highlights: Japan prioritizes rule of law and economic benefits over direct democracy promotion
Japan's cautious approach to China is influenced by geography and economics
Japan's economic aid to Southeast Asia is a form of soft power
Japan's role as a key US ally is critical in countering China's influence
Japan's approach to Southeast Asia is to listen and complement other countries' efforts
episodes.quotes: Democracy here means rule of law. You have to have predictability if you want a thriving economy. So talk about rule of law, right? You're gonna revert back to the liberal part of liberal democracy and establish the legal setup.

episodes.title: Founder-Investor Relationship: A Sincere Guide - Patrick Yip | Endgame #126
episodes.guest_title: Founder and Managing Partner, Intudo Ventures
episodes.summary: Patrick Yip discusses the importance of sincerity in the founder-investor relationship, highlighting the need for a thorough due diligence process. He emphasizes the value of building a diverse team and the importance of a long-term perspective in venture capital. Yip also shares his views on the current economic climate and the potential of Indonesia's tech sector.
key_highlights: Sincerity is essential for successful founder-investor relationships
Thorough due diligence is crucial before making investment decisions
Diversity enhances decision-making in investment teams
Indonesia's tech sector is poised for significant growth
AI is a key enabler for traditional businesses
The market correction is healthy for the ecosystem
Indonesia is a promising investment destination
episodes.quotes: “You got to be either like damn good or or you know, or or I'm really missing something here, right?”

episodes.title: Southeast Asia’s Unresolved Conflicts - Jacques Bertrand | Endgame #125 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Political Science, University of Toronto
episodes.summary: Jacques Bertrand discusses the challenges of building a sense of community in Southeast Asia, given the stark differences between countries. He contrasts Indonesia, which has a strong sense of national identity, with Myanmar, which has a history of civil war and ethnic conflict. He argues that the key to resolving these conflicts is to promote dialogue and inclusion, and to address the historical grievances of ethnic minorities.
key_highlights: ASEAN aspires to build a community of like-minded people and states
Differences between ASEAN countries are stark, making dialogue difficult
Indonesia has a strong sense of national identity due to its revolution
Military reform is necessary for political reform in Myanmar
The Philippines has a land-owning elite that creates unique challenges
Historical grievances must be addressed to build trust
A negotiated outcome is key to resolving conflict
episodes.quotes: I have admiration for Southeast Asians wanting to build a community of like-minded people and states, and ASEAN in many ways aspires to being that. But you go from one country to the next, and the differences are so stark that it's amazing that one can have that dialogue and that sense of community.

episodes.title: Target Iklim 2030 Tidak Cukup dengan Fulus - Edwin Syahruzad | Endgame #124
episodes.guest_title: CEO of PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur
episodes.summary: Edwin Syahruzad discusses his background, including his education and career path, leading to his current role as CEO of PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur. He highlights the importance of infrastructure development and the role of PT SMI in providing financing and support to local governments. He also touches on the challenges and opportunities in the energy transition, particularly in the context of Indonesia's commitment to reducing carbon emissions.
key_highlights: Early life in Jakarta and education at Kanisius
Family tradition of education
Active in sports during childhood
Economics at FE UI
Early career in banking
Joining PT SMI
PT SMI's role in infrastructure development
episodes.quotes: Karena ada matematika, ya sudah. Di situ ada Matematika I dan Matematika II, tertariklah saya dengan Ekonomi. Saya kebetulan juga ambil jurusan Ekonomi.

episodes.title: Niall Ferguson on Twitter, Elitism, and the Next World War | Endgame #123 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Senior Fellow at the Hoover Institution, Stanford University
episodes.summary: Niall Ferguson and Gita Wirjawan discuss the polycrisis, the role of elites, and the future of liberal democracy. Ferguson argues that the democratization of information has not led to greater social mobility, and that the centralization of the internet has created new forms of censorship. He also contends that the West has lost touch with its roots, and that the pursuit of power has become more important than the search for truth.
key_highlights: AI should be called "inhuman intelligence" to reflect its different form
Cognitive dissonance is the essence of human life
Woke capitalism at Davos is not genuinely believed
Football clubs as national allegiances are now run by mercenaries
Psychologists were wrong to think cognitive dissonance would make us ill
The world is not flat, and globalization is not a given
The best of America is its ethic of public service
episodes.quotes: I propose that we stop calling it 'artificial intelligence', but call it instead 'inhuman intelligence', because it's not human intelligence, it's a different form of intelligence.

episodes.title: Pengobat Krisis Konten Anak - Anggia Kharisma | Endgame #122
episodes.summary: Anggia Kharisma discusses the importance of creating quality content for children and families, emphasizing the need for a balance between education and entertainment. She argues that content should be relevant to the target audience and promote critical thinking and emotional well-being. Kharisma also highlights the importance of parental involvement and collaboration in creating a positive media environment for children.
key_highlights: Content should be relevant to the target audience and promote critical thinking
Balance education and entertainment in children's content
Parents need to be proactive in guiding children's media consumption
Importance of emotional well-being in children's development
Collaboration is key to creating quality content
Need for more content that reflects Indonesian culture
Fostering a love for reading is crucial
episodes.quotes: Kita butuh dukungan sih buat konten ini juga bisa sustain dan harapan terbesarnya harusnya umur Domikado itu lebih panjang dari umur kreatornya supaya mereka bisa.

episodes.title: Indonesia Ketua ASEAN Lagi. Masihkah Relevan? - Scot Marciel | Endgame #121 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Scot Marciel argues that while ASEAN has contributed positively, it has proven itself incapable of addressing regional problems. He suggests that Southeast Asian countries should see the US as a reliable economic partner. He also argues that the world should not pressure ASEAN to take a view that is against China.
key_highlights: ASEAN has proven itself incapable of addressing regional problems
The US should be a reliable economic partner for Southeast Asia
Southeast Asian countries should have good relations with China
The world should not pressure ASEAN to be against China
The US should invest in Southeast Asia
The US should promote democracy in Southeast Asia
episodes.quotes: I think the reality is that ASEAN for all the good that it has done, has proven itself not very capable of addressing regional problems.

episodes.title: Buru Beasiswa ke Luar Negeri, Jadikan RI Kiblat Riset - Bagus Muljadi | Endgame #120
episodes.guest_title: Assistant Professor at the University of Nottingham
episodes.summary: Bagus Muljadi discusses his journey from a non-traditional academic background to becoming a professor, emphasizing the importance of interdisciplinary research and the need for Indonesia to prioritize scientific narratives in policymaking. He contrasts Indonesia's approach to education and research with that of China, highlighting the need for a shift towards a knowledge-based economy and greater investment in education.  He also touches on the importance of fostering a culture of risk-taking and innovation within Indonesian academia.
key_highlights: Indonesia needs to prioritize scientific narratives to inform policymaking and engage stakeholders
There's a lack of incentives for academics to focus research on policy relevance
Indonesia should foster a culture of risk-taking and innovation in academia
Indonesia is the biggest invisible country, not imposing its presence globally
Indonesia needs to unlock its potential through scientific collaboration and public education
episodes.quotes: Kalau tidak ada narasi yang bagus daripada saintis untuk menginformasikan kepada pembuat kebijakan dan pemangku kepentingan bahwa ini adalah hal yang sangat relevan dan Indonesia harus punya kunci, nggak ada insentif bagi baik para akademisi maupun para pembuat kebijakan.

episodes.title: Siapa yang Diuntungkan dari Histeria Resesi? | Endgame The Take #14
episodes.guest_title: Indonesian entrepreneur, educator, and former Trade Minister
episodes.summary: Gita Wirjawan discusses the economic outlook for 2023, particularly in Indonesia, emphasizing the importance of long-term thinking and investment over consumption. He highlights the impact of social media misinformation on economic anxiety and advocates for financial literacy and diversification. He suggests that Indonesia's economy is somewhat insulated from global trends due to its lower trade-to-GDP ratio.
key_highlights: Inflation in early 2023 may be more manageable than recent months
Mindset shift needed: prioritize investment over consumption
High time preference leads to mortgaging the future for current needs
Indonesia's economy is less interconnected than other developed nations
Social media misinformation fuels economic anxiety and requires critical curation
Bitcoin is a unique asset class with limited supply and global accessibility
episodes.quotes: Semakin rendah preferensi untuk hal-hal yang terkini, semakin tinggi preferensi untuk hal-hal yang lebih bagus untuk di kemudian hari.

episodes.title: Maraton GoTo untuk Cetak 'Legacy' - Andre Soelistyo | Endgame #119
episodes.summary: Andre Soelistyo, CEO of GoTo, discusses his background and the challenges of leading a large tech company in Indonesia. He emphasizes the importance of staying ahead of technological advancements and the need for a strong organizational culture. He also touches on the unique aspects of the Indonesian market, including the need to build trust and adapt to local conditions.
key_highlights: Don't get complacent as an entrepreneur, especially in technology
Obsess over innovation and development
Engage customers because of product, not price
Companies should be sustainable and long-lasting
Engage with regulators to shape policy
Don't be afraid to challenge the status quo
episodes.quotes: Menurut saya itu jangan sampai kita juga jadi puas sebagai wirausaha, apalagi di bidang teknologi bahwa memang kita hanya mengandalkan low tech. Kita harus benar-benar terobsesi.

episodes.title: Biotechnology: Humanity’s Promising Future - Harvey Lodish | Endgame #118 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Biology and Biomedical Engineering at MIT
episodes.summary: Harvey Lodish discusses the importance of education and government funding for scientific research. He emphasizes the merging of AI and biology, and the need for Southeast Asian countries to invest in higher education to retain talent. He also touches on the ethical considerations of gene editing, advocating for somatic cell editing over germline editing.
key_highlights: Higher education and government funding are crucial for scientific advancement
AI and biology are merging, creating new opportunities
Southeast Asia needs to invest in higher education to retain talent
Gene editing should be limited to somatic cells, not germline cells
Biotech is growing exponentially, with AI playing a significant role
The future of medicine lies in long-term therapies
episodes.quotes: Look, it's all education. It's all higher education and the willingness of the governments and the politicians to put the money in there. AI and biology are merging and that will become very important.

episodes.title: Perang, Jangan Biarkan Ego Adikuasa Menang - Connie Bakrie | Endgame #117
episodes.summary: Connie Bakrie and Gita Wirjawan discuss the geopolitical landscape, focusing on the US, China, and Russia. Bakrie argues that the US is trying to move the theater of war to Asia, and that Indonesia should be wary of being drawn into this conflict. She also argues that Indonesia should not blindly follow the US, but should instead pursue its own interests.
key_highlights: Indonesia should not blindly follow the US in its foreign policy
Indonesia should focus on strengthening its maritime power
The US is trying to move the theater of war to Asia
Indonesia should be wary of being drawn into conflict
Indonesia should not see China as a threat
Russia is led by a president who is finished with himself
episodes.quotes: Indonesia sebesar sehebat segagah perkasa ini, enggak pantas untuk playing victim. Terutama hormatilah nenek moyang kita yang hebat-hebat tadi. Ini tanah air kita satu-satunya. Ini yang harus kita pertahankan dan terus kita harus survive.

episodes.title: Francis Fukuyama: Liberalism and Its Broken Promises | Endgame #116 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Visiting Fellow at Stanford University's Freeman Spogli Institute for International Studies
episodes.summary: Francis Fukuyama discusses the moral purpose of autonomy, arguing that it should not be pursued for its own sake but for the substance of what it enables. He reflects on the trajectory of liberalism, noting a recent democratic recession and the rise of undemocratic movements. He emphasizes the importance of meritocracy and the need for strong institutions to counteract the potential dangers of technology and concentrated power.
key_highlights: Autonomy should be pursued for the substance of what it enables
Autocratic governments undermine basic freedoms
Liberalism is a complex trajectory
Democratic recession in the last 15 years
Need for meritocracy
Technology is morally neutral
Need for strong institutions
episodes.quotes: People want autonomy because they don't like living under authoritarian governments and they want these basic freedoms. If you say that autonomy for its own sake is more important than the substance of what you're using your autonomy for, then I think you begin to undermine the moral purpose.

episodes.title: Keroyokan Bangun ‘Padepokan Unicorn’ - Jockie Heruseon | Endgame #115
episodes.summary: Jockie Heruseon discusses Telkomsel's digital transformation, emphasizing the importance of talent and culture alongside technology. He highlights the need for a realistic approach to innovation, focusing on profitability and strategic partnerships. He also touches on the importance of language skills for Indonesian talent to compete globally and the need for a supportive ecosystem for startups.
key_highlights: Digital transformation is not just about technology but also about people and processes
Talent is key to attracting investors and driving innovation
Digital adoption requires a focus on people and processes, not just technology
Telkomsel is building an ecosystem to support digital innovation
Indonesian talent needs to be fluent in international languages to compete globally
Telkomsel acts as a venture builder to support promising startups
episodes.quotes: Untuk bisa investor itu benar-benar melirik, ini balik ke yang masalah pertama: masalah talenta. Karena transformasi digital itu bukan hanya sekedar ngomongin teknologi. Orang, proses, metriks-metriks ngukurnya nanti, itu juga hal yang perlu dipikirkan.

episodes.title: Jeffrey Pfeffer: Harness Power for Good | Endgame #114 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Professor of Organizational Behavior at Stanford University's Graduate School of Business
episodes.summary: Jeffrey Pfeffer argues that execution, not strategy, is the key to success, and that execution requires influence. He also notes that power is like water in a mug: the more you drink, the less there is. He also argues that power is not an enduring personality trait, but something that can be learned. He also notes that the US healthcare system is an example of a system with no measurement, no accountability, and no performance.
key_highlights: Leadership is about getting things done through other people
Execution requires influence
You have to influence people to get things done
You have to get out of your own way
You have to break the rules
You have to be bold
You have to be authentic
episodes.quotes: “The difference for us is not strategy, it’s execution. And execution requires influence. I mean, that’s what leadership is. Leadership is about getting things done through other people. Unless they were going to do it anyway, you have to influence them.”

episodes.title: Nadiem Makarim: Siap Dihujat Demi Bela Generasi Berikutnya | Endgame #113
episodes.guest_title: Minister of Education, Culture, Research, and Technology of Indonesia
episodes.summary: Nadiem Makarim discusses the challenges of transforming Indonesia's education system. He highlights the importance of empowering teachers, removing unnecessary curriculum content, and promoting a culture of open discussion and critical thinking. He admits that the government's efforts to improve PISA scores have been slow, but he remains optimistic that Indonesia can become a leader in education.
key_highlights: Entering government requires a shift from agile private sector to rigid public sector
Openness to leadership is not a common culture in government
High profile role limits freedom, but impact is much greater
The most important tool for parents is time with their children
The most important thing to look for in teachers is their motivation
The goal is to create lifelong learners
The key to success is to make learning engaging and fun
episodes.quotes: I don't have any power at all with the promotion of my SDM. I don't have any power at all to the salary of my karyawan, promosi, insentif structure enggak ada sama sekali, itu dipegang kementerian lain.

episodes.title: Prof Quraish Shihab: Benahi Hati, Baca Alam Raya | Endgame #112 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Quraish Shihab argues that religious guidance is like the sun, too bright to look at directly, so reason is needed as a filter. He emphasizes that the most important thing is to increase knowledge and awareness. He also argues that Indonesia should strive for a balance between democracy and Islamic values, emphasizing the importance of deliberation and inclusivity.
key_highlights: Religious guidance is like the sun, too bright to look at directly
Reason is needed as a filter to understand religious guidance
We need black sunglasses to read the light
Nalar is the black sunglasses that helps us read without being blinded
We must understand the context in which the text was written
Islam needs to be understood in the context of the modern world
We should seek common ground, not emphasize differences
episodes.quotes: Tuntunan-tuntunan agama itu bagaikan matahari. Anda tidak bisa memahaminya karena Anda bisa silau dengan cahayanya. Maka, Anda perlu alat supaya melihat tapi tidak silau. Kita perlu kacamata hitam. Nalar itu adalah kacamata hitam yang menjadikan kita mampu membaca tanpa silau.

episodes.title: Romo Magnis: Bangsa yang Utuh, Bangsa yang Bijaksana | Endgame #111 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: In this episode, Gita Wirjawan interviews Franz Magnis-Suseno (Romo Magnis), a German-born Indonesian philosopher and Jesuit priest. Romo Magnis discusses the importance of ethical convictions in education, the value of Pancasila as a foundation for Indonesian society, and the need for critical thinking. He also reflects on his experiences in Indonesia, the evolution of Marxism, and the challenges facing democracy in the 21st century.
key_highlights: Education should move away from the "filling empty bottles" model
Ethical convictions that cannot be compromised should be instilled
Pancasila is a great foundation for attitudes that need to be adopted
Critical thinking is essential for navigating the complexities of the modern world
Democracy is in crisis due to the influence of social media
The Chinese model of governance is a potential competitor to democracy
Indonesia is particularly vulnerable to the negative effects of social media
episodes.quotes: "Pendidikan harus lepas dari model yang saya kira masih dipakai adalah model mengisi botol kosong."

episodes.title: Dr. Fahruddin Faiz: Akal Budi Tidak untuk Disia-siakan | Endgame #110
episodes.guest_title: Lecturer at UIN Sunan Kalijaga in Yogyakarta
episodes.summary: Dr. Fahruddin Faiz discusses the importance of critical thinking and the dangers of intellectual stagnation. He argues that the key to a fulfilling life lies in the willingness and ability to think for oneself, and warns against the temptation to blindly follow authority or popular opinion. He also emphasizes the need for intellectual humility and a willingness to engage with diverse perspectives.
key_highlights: The key to a fulfilling life is the willingness and ability to think.
Closing the door to thinking leads to stagnation and failure.
Akal budi (intellect) is the greatest gift from God.
Don't let the gift of intellect be neglected.
An examined life is a life worth living.
The biggest problem today is the lack of critical thinking.
episodes.quotes: Jangan lupa bahwa anugerah paling besar yang diberikan oleh Tuhan, oleh Allah pada kita, itu akal budi. Jangan sampai anugerah terbaik ini, kita diamkan, kita cueki begitu saja.

episodes.title: Sadhguru: Pencerahan Akhir Tahun | Endgame #109 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Sadhguru argues that humanity's focus on material wealth and security has led to a neglect of inner evolution, resulting in a world where powerful technologies are wielded by the unenlightened. He contends that the current educational system, designed to produce uniform individuals, hinders true human potential. He also suggests that true progress lies not in seeking salvation or inclusion but in cultivating clarity and awareness of one's mortality.
key_highlights: The greatest crime is telling people there's a better place than this
Ignorance breeds arrogance; it's why the world is violent
The goal is not security but dissolution
Law enforcement is a stop-gap, not a solution
The world is not getting darker, we're just seeing more
The purpose of education is to make one's own conclusions
The most important thing is to cultivate the society
episodes.quotes: "One of the greatest crimes that we have done in the world is to tell people that there is a better place than this."

episodes.title: Harapan Basmi Malaria dari Peneliti Indonesia - Novalia Pishesha | Endgame #108
episodes.summary: Novalia Pishesha discusses the importance of structured STEM education in Indonesia to increase interest in science and technology. She notes that there is a lack of job opportunities for STEM graduates in Indonesia, which in turn discourages students from pursuing STEM degrees. She also highlights the cultural barriers that women face in pursuing STEM careers, and emphasizes the importance of mentorship and support for women in STEM.
key_highlights: Structured STEM education is needed in Indonesia to increase interest in science
Lack of STEM job opportunities discourages students from pursuing STEM degrees
Cultural barriers discourage women from pursuing STEM careers
The process of applying for S3 in STEM is not well-known
Schooling in STEM in the US is free with a stipend
Mentorship and support are important for women in STEM
episodes.quotes: “Penting juga bahwa lebih ada strukturisasi, jadi kayak kurikulum-kurikulum yang mungkin jauh lebih meningkatkan minat di sains dan teknologi, karena kayaknya jumlah S3 di Indonesia jauh lebih kurang.”

episodes.title: Belajar dari Tuan Rumah G20 Berikutnya - Naushad Forbes | Endgame #106 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Naushad Forbes discusses India's potential, its relationship with China, and the importance of education. He argues that while colonialism was a terrible thing, India should not blame it for all its ills. He also emphasizes the need for India to invest in infrastructure and education to unlock its potential and compete with China. Forbes also highlights the importance of openness and multilateralism in a multipolar world.
key_highlights: Humility and confidence are both needed
Colonialism was terrible, but blaming it for everything won't fix problems
India must solve her own problems and do things herself
India can be as rich as South Korea if it invests in education
India needs to be more open and welcoming to attract talent
The state should be an enabler, not a doer
India's strength is its diversity
episodes.quotes: "Together with the humility, you need confidence... I don't blame colonialism and the fact that India was once a colony for all our ills."

episodes.title: Bikin Baju yang Sembuhkan Bumi - Denica Riadini-Flesch | Endgame #105
episodes.summary: Denica Riadini-Flesch discusses the importance of incorporating local wisdom, like the Indonesian concept of "tumpang sari" (intercropping), into sustainable farming practices. She argues that regenerative farming, a concept now gaining traction globally, is rooted in Indonesia's traditional agricultural practices. Denica advocates for Indonesia to embrace its unique identity in development, rather than simply emulating other countries. She also highlights the need for consumers to be aware of the environmental and social impacts of their clothing choices.
key_highlights: Kapass in Indonesia can grow together with other plants
Tumpang sari is regenerative farming
Nature-based climate solutions are the best
Indonesia should become Indonesia, not Singapore ke-2
We should not forget local wisdom
We need to be critical in our thinking
episodes.quotes: "Gimana narasi perkembangan di Indonesia bukan menjadi Singapura ke-2 atau Jerman ke-2 atau Amerika ke-2 gitu kan, tapi kita menjadi Indonesia. Kita nggak boleh lupa dengan kearifan lokal ini."

episodes.title: Is West Still Best? - Andrew Sheng | Endgame #104 (Luminaries)
episodes.guest_title: Former Chairman of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission
episodes.summary: Andrew Sheng argues that the current international monetary system is inherently unfair to emerging markets, as it is based on a dollar-centric model that benefits developed economies. He contends that the West has forgotten the rest of the world, and that emerging markets need to develop their own financial systems based on their unique circumstances. He advocates for a move towards decentralized finance (DeFi) and blockchain technology to empower individuals and communities, and to challenge the dominance of centralized financial institutions.
key_highlights: The world is completely financialized, but it is not just a financialized world
Finance has become digitized, it is a network and information
The hologram of reality is created by elites
The 1% forgets the 99%, that is when empires fall
The West has forgotten the rest of the world
We need to trust our own people
The key question is what is money?
episodes.quotes: What we have is a hologram of reality, and the trouble is that hologram is created by elites. And when the elites forget the masses, the 1% forget the 99%, that's when empires fall.

episodes.title: Kenapa Pemimpin Perempuan Masih Sedikit? - Ira Noviarti | Endgame #103
episodes.summary: Ira Noviarti, CEO of Unilever Indonesia, discusses the importance of ambition and impact in leadership. She argues that hard work alone is insufficient for career advancement, emphasizing the need to create a tangible impact on the company and community. Noviarti also touches on the unique challenges faced by women in leadership, highlighting the importance of mentorship and support systems.
key_highlights: Don't be afraid of ambition, as long as you don't bring others down
Hard work only keeps your job
Think beyond hard work; think about the impact you want to create
Companies don't know what you want; you have to tell them
Hardship can make a team stronger
The importance of diversity and inclusion in leadership
Mentorship and support systems are crucial for women in leadership
episodes.quotes: "Nothing wrong of actually being ambitious selama kita tidak menjatuhkan orang lain."

episodes.title: Sandiaga Uno - Tanah Gemah Ripah, Ramah, Bernilai Tambah | Endgame #102
episodes.guest_title: Minister of Tourism and Creative Economy of Indonesia
episodes.summary: Sandiaga Uno discusses Indonesia's tourism and creative economy, emphasizing the importance of local wisdom and digital transformation. He highlights the need for a shift towards quality and sustainable tourism, and the role of local communities in driving economic growth. He also touches on the challenges of balancing conservation with economic development, and the importance of empowering local entrepreneurs.
key_highlights: Investment in MotoGP to boost tourism and creative economy
World spotlight on Indonesia to drive transformation and productivity
Indonesia's tourism sector aims for Net Zero by 2045
Tourism and creative economy contribute 12% to Indonesia's GDP
Digitalization is key to empowering micro, small, and medium enterprises
Need to balance tourism with conservation and local community involvement
episodes.quotes: "We invested in MotoGP itu probably dengan sirkuit dan lain sebagainya mungkin all in all 2 sampai 2,7 triliun rupiah dan seperti yang gue bicara di Endgame terakhir adalah gue semakin melihat trajectory kita untuk memenangkan Oscar."

episodes.title: Sehatkan Indonesia dengan Mi Instan? - Shinta Nurfauzia | Endgame #101
episodes.summary: Shinta Nurfauzia, founder of Lemonilo, discusses her journey as an entrepreneur, the importance of having a vision, and the challenges of building a business in Indonesia. She argues that Lemonilo is more than just an instant noodle company, but a brand that promotes healthy lifestyles and empowers local communities. She also emphasizes the importance of having a strong team and a clear understanding of the market.
key_highlights: Startup success is about being a camel, not a unicorn: endurance and resilience
Courage is the only virtue you cannot fake
Lemonilo aims to be the Tesla of instant noodles
Impact is the most important metric for success
Focus on the main course, not just the appetizers
episodes.quotes: "Sekarang startup yang laku itu bukan Unicorn tapi Camel (unta). Camel artinya: banyak isinya. Dia jalannya bisa panjang."

episodes.title: Yuval Noah Harari & Mark Solms: Dawn of Future Consciousness | Endgame #100 | UWRF2022
episodes.guest_title: Historian and Professor, Hebrew University of Jerusalem; Neuroscientist and Psychoanalyst, University of Cape Town
episodes.summary: In this episode of Endgame, Yuval Noah Harari and Mark Solms discuss the evolution of consciousness, its relationship to technology, and the importance of compassion. Harari argues that humans have a unique capacity for imagination, which has led to both great achievements and terrible atrocities. Solms emphasizes the importance of understanding our emotional nature and the need to develop compassion in order to navigate the challenges of the future. Wirjawan suggests that social media amplifies negative narratives.
key_highlights: Ancient traditions can advance humanity as much as technology and science
Invest in artificial and human intelligence equally
Human minds create intersubjective realities like money and nations
Human nature has a dark side that must be managed
The like button externalizes and amplifies the need for validation
Meditation and psychoanalysis help us understand ourselves and our emotions
Compassion is essential for navigating the challenges of the future
episodes.quotes: This is an ancient tradition. It's again an example of how, you know, we don't need to look only to technology and to science in order to advance the cause of humanity and indeed of the planet as a whole.

episodes.title: Dr. Saifedean Ammous: Resesi Makin Parah Jika Hal Ini Terus Dilakukan | Endgame #99 (Luminaries)
episodes.summary: Saifedean Ammous argues that fiat currency, controlled by central banks, enables governments to manipulate the economy and devalue savings. Ammous also rejects the notion of a climate crisis, arguing that it is a fiction used to justify centralized control and resource allocation.

It's important to be a good person, not just a talented one
episodes.quotes: Ternyata untuk memperpanjang umur kita di dunia industri ini, ya kita harus mau lompat ke bawah untuk naik lagi. Orang tuh kadang lupa, dia kalau udah di atas, dia tinggal nunggu jatuh, sebenarnya.

episodes.title: Mengendalikan Ekonomi Bukan Sekedar Menggeser Tiang Gawang - Prof Iwan Jaya Azis | Endgame #70
episodes.summary: Iwan Jaya Azis discusses the lessons learned from past economic crises, particularly the Asian Financial Crisis of 1998 and the Global Financial Crisis of 2008. He emphasizes the importance of becoming a developed nation to avoid being manipulated by international organizations. He also touches on the trend of decentralization in financial systems and the rise of cryptocurrencies as a reaction to distrust in traditional establishments.
key_highlights: Becoming a developed nation is key to avoiding manipulation by international organizations
Need to learn public policy to contribute to Indonesia's future
The ego is a concept, a symbol, a delusion
The ego is like money, a concept, a symbol, a delusion
The ego is like money, a concept, a symbol, a delusion
episodes.quotes: Pelajaran adalah cobalah menjadi negara maju. Karena kalau Anda menjadi negara maju, apapun yang Anda lakukan, tidak ada, bahkan organisasi internasional seperti IMF, itu berani mengutak-atik.

episodes.title: Buka Jalan Menuju Mimpi dan Pengetahuan - Ketty Lie | Endgame #69
episodes.summary: Ketty Lie discusses her journey from North Jakarta to the University of Chicago, and how her experiences have shaped her views on education and philanthropy. She emphasizes the importance of financial literacy and critical thinking, and the need to bridge the gap between education and employment. She argues that Indonesia needs to empower its people to compete in a globalized world.
key_highlights: Broken systems persist because they benefit someone
It's delusional to think everyone wants change
Public policy understanding helps actualize solutions to structural problems
Education is a golden opportunity to build character
Indonesia needs to empower its people to compete globally
Financial awareness is key to long-term planning
Need to focus on the philosophy and not just the science
episodes.quotes: The system that exists, however broken, it is there because there are some who benefit. It's delusional to say that everyone wants to change the system.

episodes.title: Kecerdasan dan Kebahagiaan dari Perspektif Neurosains - Ryu Hassan | Endgame #68
episodes.summary: Dr. Ryu Hassan argues that wisdom is a matter of emotional rather than rational intelligence. He notes that the human body is designed for selfishness, and that emotional intelligence is about preventing emotions from hijacking rationality. He also suggests that Indonesia should focus on public policy education to contribute to a better future. He also discusses the importance of fiction and narrative in binding communities together.
key_highlights: Wisdom is a matter of emotional intelligence, not rational intelligence
The human body is designed for selfishness
Emotional intelligence is about preventing emotions from hijacking rationality
Indonesia should focus on public policy education for a better future
Rationality is often hijacked by emotion
The importance of fiction and narrative in binding communities together
episodes.quotes: Bijaksana itu adalah masalah kecerdasan emosi bukan kecerdasan rasional. Karena tubuh kita itu dirancang untuk egois memang.

episodes.title: Haris Azhar: Menjaga Kebebasan Berolah Pikir | Endgame #67
episodes.summary: Haris Azhar discusses the importance of freedom of thought and expression, and the need for a society to cultivate critical thinking. He argues that Indonesia's focus on economic development and technological advancement has come at the expense of human rights and ethical considerations. He emphasizes the importance of having a strong moral compass and the ability to challenge authority, and the need to democratize access to information and ideas.
key_highlights: Freedom of thought and expression are essential for a healthy society
Technology should serve humanity, not the other way around
Critical thinking is necessary to challenge authority and injustice
Indonesia needs to prioritize human rights and ethical considerations
Economic development should not come at the expense of human rights
The importance of having a strong moral compass
The need to democratize access to information and ideas
episodes.quotes: Apapun pekerjaan kita, kita harus punya freedom. Kita harus bisa menggunakan kebebasan itu. Kebebasan untuk berbicara. Untuk menyerap informasi. Tinggal di mana pun dia ingin. Tapi dia tahu apa yang dia mesti ambil keputusan, bahannya apa, dia cari bahannya di mana untuk dia bikin keputusan.

episodes.title: Fundamental Investasi untuk Negeri - Luky Alfirman | Endgame #66
episodes.guest_title: Director General of Risk Financing Management, Ministry of Finance, Indonesia
episodes.summary: Luky Alfirman discusses the importance of realizing Indonesia's economic potential by developing its financial markets and increasing productivity. He emphasizes the need for a balanced approach to debt management, focusing on long-term benefits and strategic allocation of resources. He also highlights the importance of financial literacy and expanding the investor base to include retail investors, while maintaining prudent fiscal policies and transparency.
key_highlights: Potential is only potential unless it is cultivated and realized
Indonesia is transitioning from a cash-based society
Government spending is for the benefit of the people
We must focus on the productivity of labor and capital
Indonesia is committed to fiscal prudence and debt management
Indonesia aims to deepen its financial markets
Indonesia is leveraging technology to improve tax administration
episodes.quotes: Kita itu punya potensi, tapi kan kita tahu potensi itu hanya potensi belaka, kalau enggak digarap/direalisasikan, ya tetap saja jadi potensi.

episodes.title: Indrawan Nugroho: Yang Jarang Dibicarakan Soal Inovasi | Endgame #65
episodes.summary: Indrawan Nugroho argues that technology is merely a tool to enhance human capabilities like creativity, productivity, and connection. He emphasizes that the essence of innovation lies in the human element, not the technology itself, and that Indonesia's rich cultural heritage can be a source of innovation if framed correctly. He suggests that Indonesia should focus on forward-looking storytelling, rather than being constrained by its past, and that innovation is about empowering people to be their best selves.
key_highlights: Technology is just a tool to enhance human creativity, productivity, and connection
Innovation is about empowering people, not just adopting new technology
Indonesia's rich cultural heritage can be a source of innovation
Indonesia needs to focus on forward-looking storytelling, not just the past
Innovation should be meaningful and move people, not just be trendy
The biggest enemy of innovation is assumption
The core of innovation is human
episodes.quotes: Teknologi itu cuman alat untuk bisa menghidupkan sesuatu dalam diri kita, apakah itu creativity, produktivity, koneksi dengan orang lain.

episodes.title: Retno Marsudi: Indonesia Bisa Jadi Jembatan Peradaban | Endgame #64
episodes.guest_title: Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Indonesia
episodes.summary: Retno Marsudi argues that Indonesia's foreign policy of being "free and active" is more relevant than ever in a polarized world. She emphasizes Indonesia's role as a bridge between civilizations, highlighting the importance of trust and collaboration in international relations. She also discusses the challenges of vaccine diplomacy and the need for a more equitable distribution of resources, while also stressing the importance of historical context in diplomacy.
key_highlights: Indonesia's foreign policy of being "free and active" is more relevant than ever
Trust is a key asset in international relations
Indonesia strives to bridge differences between nations
Indonesia is committed to an equitable distribution of vaccines
Diplomacy must be grounded in historical context
Democracy must deliver tangible benefits to its citizens
episodes.quotes: "Kadang-kadang kita semuanya lupa bahwasanya ada satu aset yang kadang-kadang kita enggak rasakan. Bagaimana negara luar melihat Indonesia, yaitu apa? Trust (kepercayaan)."

episodes.title: Theresia Gouw: Diverse Teams Make Better Decisions | Endgame #63
episodes.summary: Theresia Gouw discusses her journey from Indonesia to becoming a successful venture capitalist in the US. She emphasizes the importance of investing in oneself and betting on one's potential, and highlights the value of diverse teams in making better decisions. Gouw also touches on the potential of blockchain technology, but cautions that the regulatory framework and energy consumption are important considerations.
key_highlights: Don't be afraid to bet on yourself
You know yourself better than anyone else
Invest in yourself, it's the least scary asset
Diversity leads to better decision-making and higher returns
Blockchain is a transport layer, not just cryptocurrency
Need for transparency and consistency in regulatory rules
The importance of quality teachers over curriculum
episodes.quotes: What's the asset that you know better than any other asset class? You. Your potential. So thinking about investing in yourself, which is what I think of entrepreneurship as, should seem like the less least scary asset that you would ever invest in.

episodes.title: Kemunduran Demokrasi Hanya Sementara - Prof Jimly Asshiddiqie | Endgame #62
episodes.summary: Professor Jimly Asshiddiqie discusses the tension between the ideals of the Indonesian constitution and the realities of a market-driven economy, suggesting a need for ongoing dialogue to prevent excessive pragmatism. He argues that the role of the state in the economy is crucial and that a balance between political and economic democracy is necessary for social justice. He also expresses concern about the potential for a democratic regression due to the influence of technology and the concentration of power.
key_highlights: Liberalism in market realities traps us, necessitating continuous dialogue
The state has a significant role in the economy
There are two types of constitutions: normative and monetary
There is a need for a balance between political and economic democracy
The virtue of a superior man is wind; the virtue of the small is grass
episodes.quotes: Jadi kita ini kan terjebak dalam liberalisme realitas pasar. Maka saya suka sebut, dua macam UUD (Undang-Undang Dasar) Undang-Undang Dasar norma tertinggi, ujung-ujungnya duit, realitas tertinggi.

episodes.title: Pendiri Lembaga Eijkman Bicara Sejarah, Peran BJ Habibie dan Anggaran | Endgame Compendium #4
episodes.summary: Sangkot Marzuki discusses the history of the Eijkman Institute, emphasizing its colonial origins and the importance of basic research in biotechnology. She highlights BJ Habibie's role in recognizing biotechnology as a national priority and the need for sustainable funding models beyond government support. Marzuki argues that science transcends national borders, and collaboration is essential for progress.
