Fundamentals of leadership are the same, execution must be culturally sensitive
Solutions are internal; focus on clear diagnosis and listening
In bad situations, you don't need to explain the problem
Differentiate content from packaging when managing across cultures
episodes.quotes: "Management and particularly leadership is about asking people to do things that they don't want to do."

episodes.title: Whole Foods CEO Mackey Wants to Transform Business
episodes.summary: John Mackey discusses the evolution of Whole Foods Market, emphasizing the importance of conscious capitalism and stakeholder-oriented business models. He shares the challenges of maintaining integrity as a company scales, including the need for transparency and a commitment to a deeper purpose beyond profit maximization. Mackey also touches on the importance of a pluralistic society where different business philosophies can experiment and thrive.
key_highlights: Capitalism won the 20th-century economic war
Corporations widely perceived as greedy, selfish, and exploitative
Conscious business based on three primary principles
Need new paradigms for thinking about business and capitalism
Corporations need to market themselves better
Conscious leadership focuses on synergies, not tradeoffs
The upward spiral of caring begins with yourself
episodes.quotes: This is the potential that humanity has: the good, the true, the beautiful, and the heroic.

episodes.title: Former World Bank President: Big Shift Coming
episodes.summary: James Wolfensohn discusses the shift in economic power from the West to China and India, and the implications for the World Bank. He emphasizes the need for a new approach to development aid, one that is driven by local leadership and focused on long-term societal change. He also stresses the importance of education and the need for the West to adapt to a changing world.
key_highlights: Focus on methodology of aid, not just the amount
Local leadership is key to effective development
Balance business with non-business activities
Understand history and culture of Asia
The world is not going backwards
episodes.quotes: If it is possible for you in whatever you're doing to engage yourself in things other than the straight business course, you will find that not only does it enrich your life, but the truth of the matter is that it'll enrich your business.

episodes.title: What It Takes to Be a Remarkable Leader: John Doerr, Venture Capitalist
episodes.summary: John Doerr shares the story of Kleiner Perkins, emphasizing its service business model focused on helping entrepreneurs. He highlights the firm's initiatives in green and digital technologies, and offers career advice centered on prioritizing learning, building a strong foundation of commercial experiences, and networking. Doerr underscores the importance of building a team with diverse perspectives and the ability to confront problems directly, while maintaining integrity and a commitment to public service.
key_highlights: Kleiner Perkins is a service business, focused on helping entrepreneurs
Build a team of partners with entrepreneurial experience
Connect with and serve entrepreneurs
Prioritize learning over compensation early in your career
Build a strong foundation of commercial experiences
Cultivate a culture of drive, passion, and meritocracy
Maintain integrity
episodes.quotes: A venture capital firm could be an episodic collection of talented individuals. We want to build a service organization that can last, that can last beyond any of the partners.

episodes.title: Managing a Global Enterprise: Andrea Jung, CEO of Avon
episodes.summary: Andrea Jung shares her journey from retail to CEO of Avon, emphasizing the importance of staying true to one's values and reinvention. She recounts a pivotal moment when she chose to remain at Avon, despite a more prestigious offer, because she loved the company's mission. She emphasizes the importance of building trust and having a sense of humor, especially in challenging times. Jung also discusses Avon's unique distribution model and its commitment to women's economic empowerment.
key_highlights: Follow your compass, not your clock
Prioritize family over work when it matters
Reassess skills and reinvent yourself
The importance of government relations
The power of influence over mandates
episodes.quotes: Follow your compass, not your clock. Make this decision not from your head, but from your heart. If you don't love the work, and you all are about to go out and do some pretty extraordinary things, then you're still not doing it.

episodes.title: Wells Fargo CEO: "What I've Learned Since Business School"
episodes.summary: The speaker, the CEO of Wells Fargo, shares lessons learned since business school, emphasizing the importance of people, culture, and a focus on revenue growth. He recounts how Wells Fargo's culture helped them avoid the subprime mortgage crisis and how they approach acquisitions. He stresses the need for leaders to empower their teams, prioritize customer needs, and maintain a strong ethical compass.
key_highlights: Flexibility in career moves is more important than ever
Know what you don't want to do
Keep an open mind
People are a competitive advantage
Prioritize revenue growth
Value personal discipline and time management
Leaders point direction, not fingers
episodes.quotes: Success without fun never lasts; fun without success isn't much fun.

episodes.title: Saudi Oil CEO Abdallah Jum'ah Discusses Future of Oil
episodes.summary: Abdallah Jum'ah, President and CEO of Saudi Aramco, shares his perspective on leadership, innovation, and the future of the energy industry. He emphasizes the importance of cross-cultural understanding, ethical conduct, and a commitment to protecting the natural environment. He also discusses the importance of balancing the needs of shareholders with the needs of society, and the need for leaders to be both innovative and principled.
key_highlights: Few universities compare to Stanford in reputation and lasting impact
Energy for generations is a reference to people and reliable supply
Innovation is the watchword; it has to be harnessed
Importance of cross-cultural understanding in a globalized world
Need to question assumptions and step out of comfort zones
The most frequent failing of executives is not staying engaged
episodes.quotes: I want the cultures of all lands to be blown about my house as freely as possible. But I refuse to be blown off my feet by any.

episodes.title: Former HP Boss Faced 'Tough Choices'
episodes.summary: Carly Fiorina discusses her leadership philosophy, emphasizing authenticity and the importance of understanding people's fears to drive change. She shares anecdotes from her career, including her time at AT&T and HP, highlighting the challenges of transforming established organizations. Fiorina stresses the need for a strong value system and the importance of making tough choices, even when unpopular, to achieve long-term success. She also touches on the role of the media in shaping perceptions of leaders and organizations.
key_highlights: Authenticity requires revealing personal details, even when controversial
Leadership is about seeing possibilities in people and circumstances
Fear is a principal motivator in business
Business is about people, not just profits and products
Culture and values are the "software" of a company
Meritocracy is difficult to build due to human nature
Leaders must be aware of resistance to change
episodes.quotes: "Leadership is all about seeing the possibilities in people and seeing the possibilities in circumstances."

episodes.title: N. R. Narayan Murthy: Do As You Say to Gain Credibility
episodes.guest_title: Founder & Executive Chairman, Infosys Technologies Limited
episodes.summary: N.R. Narayana Murthy shares lessons learned as CEO of Infosys, emphasizing the importance of people, aspirations, and ethical leadership. He recounts the early days of Infosys, highlighting the challenges of operating in a highly regulated environment and the transformative impact of India's economic reforms. Murthy defines globalization as sourcing capital, talent, and production where it's most efficient, and selling where markets are most profitable. He stresses the need for leaders to be credible, trustworthy, and to inspire their teams to embrace change and pursue ambitious goals.
key_highlights: Use context as opportunity, not constraint
A plausible impossibility is better than a convincing possibility
Value system is key to instill trust
Be the change you want to see in the world
A leader must raise aspirations of people
Credibility is built through leadership by example
episodes.quotes: Most people see things as they are and wonder why; I dream of things that never were and say why not.

episodes.title: Jack Welch: Create Candor in the Workplace
episodes.summary: Jack Welch discusses the importance of candor, differentiation, and succession planning. He emphasizes the need for leaders to be honest with their teams, reward top performers, and help underperformers find a better fit. Welch shares his belief that a company's culture should be built on a foundation of trust and transparency, and that leaders should prioritize building talent over short-term financial gains.
key_highlights: Winning is about defining clear objectives and pursuing them with everything you've got
Candor is essential for building trust and improving performance
Differentiate between high and low performers to build a strong team
Succession planning is crucial for long-term success
Reward values in the soul and the wallet
Build an organization where people feel comfortable with each other
Don't be afraid to put it out there
episodes.quotes: "This book is trying to codify some things that I learned in business in response to these questions. So this book hopefully is about you, and hopefully about audiences and some of the things that people have been challenged by."

episodes.title: Herb Kelleher: Managing in Good Times and Bad
episodes.summary: Herb Kelleher discusses Southwest's unique approach to labor relations, emphasizing partnership over adversarial dynamics. He shares an anecdote about a mechanic who found a way to add a seat to the plane, showcasing the company's culture of empowering employees. Kelleher also highlights the importance of a broad worldview for leaders and recounts a story about a pilot who was upset that the company was not doing well, and Kelleher told him to go out and sell more tickets.
key_highlights: Prioritize employees, then customers, then shareholders
Bureaucracy and hierarchy are enemies of success
Innovation requires an open mind and diverse perspectives
Acknowledge and address employee concerns directly
Success requires a passion for serving others
Don't take yourself too seriously, have fun
Be prepared for crises and recover quickly
episodes.quotes: "We manage in good times so that we'll do well in bad times."

episodes.title: Google's Kordestani talks passion
episodes.summary: Omid Kordestani shares his career journey and insights on leadership. He emphasizes the importance of following one's passion over money, and building a portfolio of learning and relationships. He recounts his early career, the challenges of building Google's international operations, and the importance of being open to surprises. He also shares advice he received from John Doerr and Trip Hawkins.
key_highlights: Follow your passion, not just the money
Build a portfolio of learning and relationships
Be open to surprises
Recruit greatness, don't compromise
Revenue illuminates the path in a startup
episodes.quotes: "Leadership can be learned... that's the only thing I remember from that class."

episodes.title: Unconventional Strategy Led to '04 Win for Mehlman
episodes.summary: Ken Mehlman shares leadership lessons learned from his career in politics, including his time as Chairman of the Republican National Committee. He emphasizes the importance of planning, sharing strategy, and using data to make decisions. He also discusses the importance of empowering people, being generous, and focusing on the needs of the organization over personal gain. He shares anecdotes from his political career to illustrate these points.
key_highlights: Plan well to enable quick action
Share strategy to convert rivals into allies
Be generous, serve others
Use data to make decisions
Be ethical, follow the law
Focus on what you can control
episodes.quotes: If you have to choose between losing or breaking the law, lose. There's nothing more important than doing things that are consistent with the law and consistent with ethics.

episodes.title: William Wendell of J&J: Adding Products Is Key
episodes.summary: William Weldon, Chairman of Johnson & Johnson, discusses the company's strategic principles, emphasizing the importance of decentralization and a focus on customers. He shares insights on leadership development, emphasizing the value of varied experiences and the ability to learn from mistakes. He highlights J&J's commitment to innovation and its efforts to balance short-term returns with long-term investments. He also discusses the company's approach to healthcare affordability and access, and the importance of working with governments and other stakeholders to address these challenges.
key_highlights: Decentralization allows for faster decision-making and empowers employees
Prioritizing customers and employees leads to long-term shareholder value
Innovation requires a willingness to challenge the status quo
Diversity in skills and backgrounds fosters better problem-solving
Learning from failures is essential for growth
Setting aggressive goals drives innovation
Healthcare should be elevated above political issues
episodes.quotes: It's all about the people. It's about being in great markets and having great people. And if you have both of those and you put them together and you allow people to really kind of spread their wings and do the things that are important and stay focused on their customer, great things happen.

episodes.title: Bewkes Wanted Different Experiences
episodes.summary: The speaker shares his career journey, including his time at HBO and Time Warner. He emphasizes the importance of honesty and transparency in leadership, especially when making difficult decisions. He also discusses the need for a culture of debate and course correction within organizations. He shares an anecdote about Christina Onassis and the importance of knowing the names of your colleagues' children.
key_highlights: View from the top can feel like the bottom
Aim your ambition
Honesty and transparency in leadership
Importance of a culture of debate
Don't just manage up
Know your colleagues' children
episodes.quotes: "I sit here all day trying to persuade people to do the thing they ought to have sense enough to do without my persuading them."

episodes.title: Leadership a "Human Skill"
episodes.summary: The CEO of DaVita discusses the importance of building a strong community within an organization, emphasizing that it should come before the company itself. He shares the story of how DaVita's name, logo, and core values were chosen by its employees. He stresses the importance of honesty and transparency in leadership, even when it means admitting to mistakes and shortcomings. He also emphasizes the need for leaders to be personally secure and to serve the people they lead.
key_highlights: Community first, company second: creating a special community
Involve employees in key decisions like choosing company name
Honesty and transparency are crucial for effective leadership
Leaders must be personally secure to face the truth
Acknowledge and own mistakes to build trust
Serve the cause, serve the people, or both
Leadership is in the eyes of the led
episodes.quotes: Leadership is a human skill. Therefore to become a better manager you work on developing better business skills. Leadership is a human skill.

episodes.title: Airline Defied Conventional Wisdom
episodes.summary: The CEO of Continental Airlines shares his experience turning around the company in the 1990s. He emphasizes the importance of a clear plan, open communication, and treating employees with dignity and respect. He recounts how Continental's decision to focus on on-time performance, even when it meant cutting flights, ultimately improved customer satisfaction and profitability. He also stresses the need to constantly reinvent the business and not take success for granted.
key_highlights: Focus on value-added costs, not just cutting costs
Communicate a simple plan to all employees
Treat employees with dignity and respect
Don't take success for granted
Constant reinvention is key
Balance is important in people's lives
episodes.quotes: "The trick isn't how do you cut costs that are value added, it's how do you cut inefficiencies, how do you cut places where you aren't getting a return."

episodes.title: Steve Ellis of Bain: Career Path Guided by Motivation
episodes.summary: Steve Ellis, Bain's Worldwide Managing Director, shares his perspective on leadership, emphasizing the importance of authenticity, courage, and a strong organizational culture. He recounts a humorous anecdote from his early days as a leader, involving a tea ceremony gone wrong, to illustrate the importance of authenticity. Ellis also stresses the need for leaders to be present and to act consistently with the values of their organization.
key_highlights: Authenticity is about knowing who you are
Courage is essential to innovation
Servant leadership model: inclusion, inquiry, insight, influence, incentives
Culture is defined by the behaviors of leaders
Leaders must manage the volume of activity
Acknowledge and learn from failures
episodes.quotes: I have to lead through a combination of inclusion, inquiry, insight, influence, and incentives. If it doesn't begin with 'in,' I can't do it.

episodes.title: George Roberts: Don't Miss Opportunities
episodes.summary: George Roberts, co-founder of KKR, shares his journey of leaving a comfortable banking job to start a new firm in an industry that didn't exist. He emphasizes the importance of making management partners in the business, rather than just employees. He also shares that the most important thing is to have a strong sense of what's important in life, and to have a supportive partner.
key_highlights: Borrowing money to buy companies is not new
Making management partners in what you do is
Don't overthink it, find a job you can contribute to
Learn more from failures than successes
Don't penalize failure, as long as you fail honorably
Find something you love to do
Focus on what's important in life
episodes.quotes: The system we have in this country does not penalize failures as long as you fail honorably. The system will give you a second and a third and a fourth chance.

episodes.title: Change and Transition to be Embraced
episodes.summary: Steve Ballmer discusses how Microsoft has adapted to changing technology and business models, emphasizing the importance of embracing new approaches and pushing for innovation. He shares a story about his decision to join Microsoft, highlighting the importance of knowing what you're good at and taking calculated risks. He also discusses the importance of balancing shareholder interests with social responsibility.
key_highlights: Ability to embrace new approaches is key to business success
Culture is important, but you have to push it
It's important to have a balance of stakeholders
Don't discount the size of the opportunity
The proof is in the execution
You need to be able to lead and fast follow
Don't get stuck in the way you built your initial success
episodes.quotes: "What we're good at is building software and commercializing it. And unless we're willing to sort of push, push for new models of software delivery, we can't keep moving and growing."

episodes.title: Brenda Barnes: Role Reversal for Women
episodes.summary: Brenda Barnes discusses the importance of leadership in times of economic crisis, noting that CEO credibility is at an all-time low. She shares her unconventional career path, including working at the post office, and emphasizes the importance of treating people with dignity, regardless of their role. Barnes also reflects on her decision to leave PepsiCo to spend time with her family, and the challenges of re-entering the workforce.
key_highlights: CEO credibility is at an all-time low
Leaders should inspire people to act and do the right thing
Bring dignity and fun to tedious jobs
Bad examples are not the ones to follow
Balance long-term vision with short-term pressures
Good governance starts with the board and the CEO
Culture is the hardest thing to change
episodes.quotes: You have two ears and one mouth, use them in that proportion

episodes.title: Jeff Raikes: CEO Must Link Values and Leadership
episodes.summary: Jeff Raikes shares how his upbringing on a Nebraska farm instilled a strong work ethic and values. He recounts his career at Microsoft, emphasizing the importance of strategy and the power of software. Raikes stresses the need for leaders to prioritize their personal lives and to be open to opportunities, even when they deviate from a plan. He concludes with a story about joining the Gates Foundation, highlighting the importance of data and partnerships to achieve ambitious goals.
key_highlights: Values shape management and leadership
Work ethic learned on the farm
Passion for what you do is key
Importance of community and helping neighbors
Internal competitiveness is more important than external
Be open to opportunities
Strategy is key to success
episodes.quotes: "It's your values that shape a lot of what's important to you as a manager or leader and also then sets the tone for the environment that you're responsible for."

episodes.title: Eric Schmidt of Google: Change Creates Opportunity
episodes.summary: Eric Schmidt shares his perspective on the political, business, and technological environments, emphasizing that change creates opportunity. He discusses the importance of transparency in government and business, and how technology can facilitate this. Schmidt also touches on the power of user-generated content and the shift towards cloud computing, highlighting the need for new business models that embrace abundance rather than scarcity.
key_highlights: Change happens when things are hard, creating phenomenal opportunities
Assets are cheap, expectations are realistic
Genius of the American system is that it corrects
Financial innovation precedes financial regulation
Real value is created by building real businesses
Transparency is the best defense against bad decisions
episodes.quotes: The genius of the American system is that it corrects. Whatever you think of the previous structure, it's just being corrected big time.

episodes.title: Entrepreneurial Spirit Needed: Tony Hayward, British Petroleum
episodes.summary: Tony Hayward, CEO of BP, shares his leadership philosophy with Stanford MBA students. He emphasizes the importance of passion, persistence, and taking calculated risks in one's career. He also shares the importance of staying grounded and connected to people. Hayward discusses BP's long-term commitment to alternative energy and the challenges of balancing profitability with environmental responsibility.
key_highlights: Follow your heart and be passionate about what you do
Invest the time to do it really well
Be persistent; nothing comes easy
Take personal risks
Stay down to earth and connected
Duty of care for everyone in the organization
Create clarity for people
episodes.quotes: "If you live each day as your last, someday you'll be right."

episodes.title: Fox Sees Bright Future for Mexico
episodes.summary: Former Mexican President Vicente Fox addresses the Stanford Graduate School of Business, discussing the importance of education, microfinance, and the need for multilateralism in addressing global challenges. He emphasizes the importance of a strong middle class and the need for Latin America to build bridges between the developed and underdeveloped world. Fox also touches on the drug war in Mexico, highlighting the need for a shared responsibility between Mexico and the United States.
key_highlights: There is life after the presidency
Importance of education and microfinance
Need for multilateralism to address global challenges
Importance of a strong middle class
Build bridges between developed and underdeveloped world
Shared responsibility between Mexico and the United States
episodes.quotes: The antidote against demagoguery, against populism, it's education, and it is information and it is middle class.

episodes.title: Chevron CEO: Energy 'Not a God-Given Right'
episodes.summary: Chevron CEO Dave O'Reilly discusses the importance of energy literacy and the need for a pragmatic approach to climate change. He emphasizes that a large-scale shift to renewables is unlikely in the near future, highlighting the need for a diverse energy portfolio. O'Reilly also shares his leadership philosophy, emphasizing the importance of integrity and the ability to work effectively with diverse teams and governments.
key_highlights: Energy is not a god-given right; it must be produced and used carefully
The world's energy system is enormous and complex
A 60% reduction in emissions by 2050 is unlikely
The oil industry is under fire, but must communicate facts
It's important to be able to work with diverse teams
The importance of integrity in leadership
Focus on long-term supply and geopolitical risk
episodes.quotes: When you're from a small country, you learn to look outward.

episodes.title: Wendy Kopp: Fill Rooms with Committed Teachers
episodes.summary: Wendy Kopp shares the story of founding Teach for America, starting with the insight that there was no national recruiting effort to attract top college graduates to teaching in low-income communities. She emphasizes the power of inexperience, the importance of sticking with something long enough to learn the lessons, and the need to focus on the work itself. She highlights the importance of building local capacity and the challenges of scaling a mission-driven organization.
key_highlights: The power of inexperience to set ambitious goals
The importance of sticking with something
The value of time and perseverance
Focus on the work, not on leadership
Build local capacity for sustained change
Reject silver bullet solutions
Cultivate a culture of relentless pursuit of results
episodes.quotes: I think it took getting there for inspiration to strike, you know, one day I just thought, why aren't we being recruited as aggressively to teach in our country's lowest income communities as we were being recruited at the time to work on Wall Street?

episodes.title: Dean Robert Joss: Leadership Means Responsibility
episodes.guest_title: Dean, Stanford Graduate School of Business
episodes.summary: Dean Robert Joss defines leadership as taking responsibility for a group with a mission. He shares that the most difficult leadership moments involve making tough choices that may cause short-term pain for some, but are ultimately the right thing for the group. He emphasizes the importance of a leader being the embodiment of the group's values and vision, and communicating that vision effectively.
key_highlights: Leadership is taking responsibility for a group with a mission
Leaders must have a clear vision
Leaders must embody the group's values
Communication is key to effective leadership
Moral courage is essential for tough decisions
It's about 'them,' not 'you'
Leaders must invite open criticism
episodes.quotes: Being powerful is like being a lady. If you have to say you are, you aren't.

episodes.title: Approach to Leadership Evolves: Jose Antonio Fernandez, FEMSA
episodes.summary: Jose Antonio Fernandez Carbajal, Chairman & CEO of FEMSA, shares his leadership journey, emphasizing the importance of mentorship and learning from mistakes. He recounts a pivotal lesson from his father-in-law: the need to control a company's commercial agenda to ensure long-term value. He discusses FEMSA's diversification strategy, highlighting the challenges of balancing expansion with core competencies. He also emphasizes the importance of treating employees well and creating a positive company culture.
key_highlights: Great mentors are crucial for leadership development
Control your commercial agenda for long-term value
Never impose decisions; empower your team
Prioritize long-term value over short-term gains
Transparency and good governance are essential
Diversification should align with core competencies
episodes.quotes: "The minute you feel very safe in what you do, the minute you are starting not to make your effort, that's the natural human thing."

episodes.title: SEIU's Stern to Unions: "Join the Economic Revolution"
episodes.summary: Andy Stern, Int'l President of SEIU, shares his perspective on the economic revolution and the role of unions. He emphasizes the importance of finding meaning in one's career and following one's passion. Stern recounts his decision to not become a lawyer, despite family expectations, and the importance of collective bargaining to level the playing field for workers. He also discusses the need for unions to adapt to the changing economic landscape.
key_highlights: Meaning matters more than money or status in career choices
Find your passion and follow your heart
Unions must adapt to the changing economic landscape
Unions should call out bad business decisions
Level the playing field for fair competition
episodes.quotes: There is no substitution for doing something that means something to you in life. May not mean anything to your mother or your father or your spouse at times, it may cause lots of consternation.

guests.name: Multiple Guest
guests.title: Dr. Hendri Saparini, Tirza Munusamy, Dee Lestari

guests.name: Sarah Paine
